name: kittooltip
description: Project containing your PowerApps Component Framework (PCF) control.

comments.virtual: This is a virtual control: the platform hands it the host's own React and Fluent at runtime (the manifest's platform-library lines), so neither is bundled and no tabster question arises at all: there is exactly one focus-management instance on the page, the host's. React and Fluent live in devDependencies purely for compile and local harness use, React at the platform version, Fluent at the kit's API floor (see pcfs/platform-floor.json), which is higher than the version the manifest declares: the manifest number is only what the org must ACCEPT at import (the runtime serves its current copy), while the devDependency records the oldest delivery the kit's code actually works against. Compiling against it keeps the control off APIs an older target org might not serve.
comments.icons: The icon package is not part of the platform Fluent library, so it stays a real dependency; the bundle carries just the icons this control imports.
